exemplar.title: Optimizing the volume of the keys in an irregularly shaped 60% mechanical keyboard

detailedExemplarData.title: Optimizing the volume of the keys in an irregularly shaped 60% mechanical keyboard

subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear logical development linking introduction, calculations and optimisation
Well-structured framework with headings, numbered subsections and a conclusion
Descriptive title and labelled figures support reader navigation
weaknesses: Occasional long digressions and redundant narrative weaken conciseness
Appendix not clearly separated from main text
Some bibliographic inconsistencies and informal headers detract from polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates coherence and logical development (band 3) and is well organized with clear structure (band 3). However, repeated explanations and narrative digressions reduce conciseness (band 2). On balance, a score of 3/4 best reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Mostly consistent and correct use of mathematical notation and terminology
Effective integration of diagrams, screenshots and tables
Logical sequencing of algebraic steps with interpretations in context
weaknesses: Minor notation slips (omitted âdx,â superscript formatting errors)
Occasional algebraic jumps without full working
Some images are small or blurry and interrupt clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are used correctly in the majority of cases (band 3), representations are effective (band 3) and the logical structure is clear overall (band 3). The occasional clarity lapses and notation errors preclude a band 4, so 3/4 is appropriate.

strengths: Strong personal context with self-taken photographs and a genuine real-world motivation
Evidence of initiative in selecting and modeling individual key types
Clear personal approach connecting the problem to travel and ergonomics
weaknesses: Mathematical techniques are largely standard and guided by software
Independent thinking is significant but not exceptional
Creativity good but does not reach an innovative methodological breakthrough
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach (band 2) with good creativity (band 2). It lacks the level of originality and depth required for band 3, so a score of 2/3 is justified.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on methodological limitations and equipment constraints
Thoughtful analysis of results and acknowledgment of the failed base optimisation
Clear evaluation of outcomes and discussion of implications for 3D printing and cost
weaknesses: Reflection is not consistently deep or substantial throughout
Critical analysis is thoughtful but not profoundly insightful
Future implications are discussed but without a detailed further-investigation plan
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student provides meaningful reflection (band 2), thoughtful analysis (band 2) and clear evaluation of outcomes (band 2). The work does not reach the sustained critical depth for band 3, so 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Relevant calculus and piecewise functions applied correctly across multiple key types
Thorough use of integration and extrusion formulae to meet investigative aims
Strong understanding demonstrated by consistent results and correct composite integrals
Effective application of mathematical techniques within the SL syllabus
weaknesses: Minor errors in unit notation, rounding consistency and a base-optimisation miscalculation
Techniques remain within standard SL scope without HL-level complexity
Some repetitive steps and occasional lack of explicit formula statements
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematics is used effectively to achieve the task (band 4 relevance), is mostly at the correct course level (band 3 level appropriateness) and shows good understanding with minor errors (band 4 accuracy). Together, these justify a score of 5/6 for Use of Mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations accompany each algebraic step, integrals are evaluated with limits shown, and results are interpreted in context.  Occasional algebraic jumps (e.g. large numerical substitutions without working) reduce absolute clarity, yet overall communication follows a logical path and is easy to trace.

Working is mainly correct, and numerical answers are consistent across sections.  Minor slips (unit formatting, rounding inconsistencies, and the incorrect base optimisation derivative) show that accuracy is not flawless, but understanding of the successful parts is thorough and errors are isolated, corresponding to âgood understanding and mostly correctâ.

Photographs, Desmos screenshots, labelled diagrams and tables are integrated into the narrative and help the reader visualise the key shape and the calculus set-up.  While some images are small or blurry, the variety and general appropriateness of representations make the use âeffectiveâ.

The student structures the report in a recognizable sequence (introduction â process â calculation â reflection).  Calculations per key type are built on one another and the optimisation section clearly refers back to earlier results, so the logical thread is visible.  However, the narrative is occasionally interrupted by long digressions (personal anecdotes, repeated explanations of the same technique) that weaken the chain of reasoning and create minor jumps, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellentâ standard of seamless development.

Using Desmos interpolation on photographs and applying an extrusion concept to physical keys shows creativity, but the optimisation itself remains a straightforward quadratic fit and volume comparison.  The level of originality is good but not âexceptionalâ.

Results (volume reduction of 56.49 cmÂ³) are interpreted in the context of manufacturing cost and portability, and the failed base optimisation is analysed.  This constitutes thoughtful, but not profoundly âinsightfulâ, critical analysis.

The student evaluates the outcome against the original aim and discusses future implications (3-D printing, alternative key spacing).  The evaluation is clear and considers implications, yet lacks a detailed plan for further investigation that would lift it to the top band.

The student comments on limitations (photo angle, ignored tilt) and explains why the base could not be optimised, demonstrating reflection that is woven through conclusion and earlier sections.  While these reflections are meaningful, they are not deep enough to be considered âsubstantialâ throughout.

Mathematical terminology (piecewise function, definite integral, extrusion) and notation for functions, domains and integrals are used correctly in the majority of cases.  Minor slips (super-script formatting, occasional omission of dx) occur but do not hinder comprehension, so use is âmostly consistentâ rather than perfect.

The work is clearly driven by the studentâs own context (travel convenience) and includes self-taken images and constraints chosen to match personal comfort.  This constitutes a âclear personal approachâ, though not an innovative methodology.

Most paragraphs are relevant to the stated aim and unnecessary background is limited, yet sections such as the two-page rationale and repeated description of extrusion inflate the length (17 pages) and lead to redundancy.  Hence the work is âmostly relevantâ rather than fully concise.

Calculus (definite integrals), piecewise functions, domain restrictions and volumetric calculations are all relevant to the task and are applied correctly in multiple contexts (regular keys, special keys).  Some steps are repetitive but overall the mathematics is used effectively to achieve the stated goal.

Choosing a personally owned keyboard, photographing individual keys and deciding to model each type separately show initiative and personal decision-making that goes beyond a textbook problem.  Still, the mathematical techniques themselves are standard and guided heavily by software, so the evidence of independent mathematical thinking is âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ.

There is a clear overall framework with headings, numbered subsections and a final conclusion.  Figures are labelled and usually referenced in the text.  Some placements (e.g. very small screenshots in the appendix) are awkward and the appendix is not clearly separated from the main discussion, but the reader can still follow the organisation without serious difficulty.

The techniques employed (basic integration of quadratics/linear functions, volume by extrusion) are squarely within the AA SL syllabus and do not extend into HL content such as multivariable optimisation or advanced calculus.  Their application is appropriate but not âfully commensurateâ with HL expectations.

annotations.comment: The 3D and 2D diagrams effectively illustrate the extrusion concept and enhance the readerâs spatial understanding.
The list of constraints is lengthy; consider presenting them in a compact table to improve readability and reference.
Introduce the formula $$V = A \,w$$ explicitly when describing extrusion to link theory and calculation clearly.
The explanation of piecewise functions is vague; include the explicit piecewise definition with intervals to improve clarity.
The composite integral is split correctly into three segments, reflecting a good grasp of the integration strategy.
Use a summation notation $$\sum$$ to represent the sum of integrals over each segment for brevity and mathematical precision.
Include unit analysis explicitly (mmÂ²ÃmmâmmÂ³) when multiplying area by width to reinforce dimensional understanding.
The visualization of spacebar extrusion applies the same methodology consistently and aids comparison with regular keys.
The clear and descriptive title effectively conveys the focus of the exploration and sets reader expectations.
The 'Pages: 17' line is an administrative detail; move it out of the main text to avoid disrupting the academic narrative.
Consider streamlining the administrative header 'Internal Assessment' to focus reader attention on the mathematical content.
The opening paragraph contains marketing language and clichÃ©s; condense to the essential context and mathematical purpose for conciseness.
Personal photographs of the keyboard are well integrated and enhance engagement with the studentâs unique context.
Figure references use inline math notation for 'Figures 1 & 2'; ensure consistent citation style (e.g., 'see Figure 1 and Figure 2').
The rationale section is overly narrative; focus on the mathematical objectives and trim personal backstory for improved coherence.
The sentence ends abruptly after 'online graphing calculator'; complete the description of the methodology to ensure clarity.
The linear segment definitions with precise domain restrictions exhibit clear and accurate mathematical communication.
Simplify fractional constants (e.g., $$\frac{19545}{169}$$) to decimals or factor common terms to improve readability of the integral result.
Clarify the origin of the constant 11.3 in the model $$f(x)=11.3$$ by explaining how it was determined from the Desmos fit.
The line 'of = 1275.68' seems to be a typographical error; correct the label to avoid confusion in volume calculation.
The constraint equation $$f(15)=7$$ clearly links the model to its geometric constraint, enhancing logical coherence.
Summarize the constraint points (2,7), (15,7), (8.5,6) in a table to reduce repetitive text and improve reader navigation.
Discuss the regression outputâs RÂ²=1 and any residuals in the table to demonstrate insight into the interpolationâs accuracy.
Present the full piecewise definition $$h(x)=\{j(x), f(x), i(x)\}$$ in one place to reinforce clarity on function domains.
Begin the conclusion with a concise numerical summary of initial and optimized volumes to reinforce the key findings.
Detailing methodological limitations and the learning journey illustrates rich critical reflection on the investigative process.
The discussion of aspectâratio constraints for the keyboard base lacks a clear equation; link length and width explicitly with a formula.
Reflect on why the derivative solution $$h\approx24.14\text{ mm}$$ falls outside the practical design constraints and propose how to enforce realistic bounds.
The candid acknowledgment of the baseâoptimization error demonstrates thoughtful reflection on methodological limitations.
Identifying how optimized keyboard models could reduce material costs and enable 3D printing links outcomes to realâworld implications.
Bibliographic entries vary in style and lack hanging indents; adopt a consistent referencing style (e.g., APA) for presentation polish.
